name: Church of Bom Jesus
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
description: An exquisite 16th-century church inside Moti Daman Fort, renowned for its stunning wooden altarpieces, intricately carved portals, and exceptional Portuguese colonial architecture.
history: Constructed in 1559 and consecrated in 1603, it stands as one of the most prominent monuments of Portuguese heritage in Daman, surviving centuries in remarkable condition.
famousFor: Portuguese architecture, intricately carved wooden altars.
